normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (a : Array Int) (n : Nat), n = a.size → True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input slice/array has a well-defined length n; no additional ordering precondition is required.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∃ (m : Nat), True ∧ (∀ i : Nat, i < n → Acc (fun j => j) i)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The insertion-sort loops terminate (e.g., by using a decreasing measure such as the outer index and/or inner index).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (i : Nat), i ≤ n →
  let a' := state_after_outer i in
  (Sorted (a'.toList.take i)) ∧
  (Permutation a'.toList (a0.toList))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: After completing the outer loop for index i, the prefix of length i is sorted and the entire array is a permutation of the original input.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (i j : Nat), i < n → j ≤ i →
  let a' := state_during_inner i j in
  (Sorted (a'.toList.take j)) ∧
  (∀ k, k < j → a'[k] ≤ a'[j]) ∧
  (Permutation a'.toList (a0.toList))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: During the inner loop (shifting elements to insert the key), the already-processed part remains sorted, the key is being moved left correctly, and the array remains a permutation of the original.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (i : Nat), i < n →
  let key := a0[i] in
  let a' := state_after_outer (i+1) in
  (∃ p, a'.toList = (a'.toList.take p) ++ [key] ++ (a'.toList.drop (p+1))) ∧
  (∀ t < p, a'[t] ≤ key) ∧
  (∀ t > p, key ≤ a'[t])
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: When inserting the element at position i (the key), it is placed at the correct position relative to the sorted prefix: all elements before are ≤ key and all after are ≥ key.
The algorithm only reorders elements; it does not change the multiset of values.
After the algorithm finishes, the entire array/slice is sorted in nondecreasing order.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (idx : Nat), idx < n →
  (final_a[idx] is defined) ∧
  (the algorithm does not allocate/modify any memory outside the slice/array bounds)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The function mutates only the given slice/array in place and does not affect anything outside its bounds.
The function returns nothing (unit) and all observable effects are the in-place mutation.
